title: polypodium castaneum
intro: Polypodium castaneum, also known as Cast Iron Fern, is a species of fern native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant's rhizomes are used in herbal medicine, with medicinal properties attributed to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Polypodium castaneum include treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, as well as acting as a natural remedy for fever reduction and pain relief. In herbal preparations, the rhizomes are often dried and used in teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
